title: Formal playable product recovery roadmap
summary: Replace milestone-count progress with a capability-gated product recovery roadmap that turns the existing simulation foundation into one understandable, continuously running, saveable colony-sim vertical slice before any release-readiness closeout.
acceptance: Roadmap formally distinguishes historical M0-M8 capability evidence from current product playability and release readiness
Roadmap defines ordered product-recovery phases with player outcome, architecture scope, dependencies, non-goals, measurable exit gates and stop signs

Roadmap requires one continuously running authoritative GameSession, perceptible autonomous residents, real input, general build/order flow, authoritative save/load and a reviewer-observed 30-minute vertical slice before release-gate re-entry
Roadmap defines evidence policy that real user input and player-observable behavior cannot be substituted by synthetic canvas dispatch, debug payloads or static fixture prose
Roadmap records disposition of WM-0154 through WM-0156 and prevents legacy closeout semantics from overriding the new product gates
No product implementation, public release, EA, store, signing, telemetry, account, paid-service, public save-compatibility or platform-verdict change is executed

history.details: thread=Beacon formal playable product recovery roadmap

Drafted the authoritative PR-0 through PR-7 playable-product recovery roadmap; preserved M0-M8 as historical evidence; blocked legacy WM-0154 evidence closeout; kept WM-0155/0156 behind the blocker; created only proposed WM-0162 for integrated GameSession architecture planning; handoff, taskctl, diff and full quality gates pass.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified PR-0 roadmap preserves M0-M8 as historical evidence, hard-gates PR-1..PR-7 on integrated GameSession, real input, continuous 30 TPS, perceptible NPCs, general building, authoritative save/load and 30-minute observed play, keeps WM-0154 blocked and WM-0155/0156 plus WM-0162 non-executing, and preserves Web demo-only, Windows controlled-test and Owner gates. validate/status/handoff/diff/quality pass.

forbiddenPaths: Simulation Worker or public protocol changes
save schema or persistence implementation changes
React, Pixi or Electron implementation changes
bulk creation of downstream implementation task packets beyond the single next-phase planning entry
public release, Early Access, store, signing, telemetry, accounts, paid services or public save compatibility actions
changing Web demo-only or Windows controlled-test verdicts without Owner approval
threadLabel: Beacon formal playable product recovery roadmap
